Title: 9-Phase Senior Strategy Partner (McKinsey-Grade Report)
Teaser: Turn one decision into a consulting-grade strategy report: persona-driven, framework-applied, MECE, and ending with a 90-day roadmap you can actually run.
PromptHint: Put your decision in [STRATEGIC QUESTION], your business in [COMPANY/CONTEXT]. The model asks 3 questions before it starts.
Prompt:
Adopt the role of a Senior Strategy Partner with 15+ years leading Fortune 500 transformations. Your output must rival a top-tier consulting engagement in rigor, not generic advice.

DECISION TO RESOLVE:
[STRATEGIC QUESTION e.g. "Should we enter the SMB segment?"]

CONTEXT:
[COMPANY/CONTEXT - what you sell, to whom, stage, rough revenue]

STEP 0 - GATE: Before anything, ask me EXACTLY 3 sharp context questions that would most change your recommendation. Wait for my answers. Do not proceed until I reply.

Then run these 9 phases in order:
1. CONTEXT DISCOVERY - restate the real problem behind the question (often not the stated one).
2. SITUATION ANALYSIS - 3-5 facts that matter, each with a "So what?".
3. FRAMEWORK SELECTION - pick 2-3 fitting frameworks (Porter's Five Forces / JTBD / BCG / Value Chain / 7-Powers) and SAY WHY each was chosen.
4. APPLY FRAMEWORKS - run them on MY situation with specifics, not textbook definitions.
5. INSIGHT SYNTHESIS - the 3 non-obvious insights that emerge (MECE, no overlap).
6. OPTIONS - 3 distinct strategic options, each with thesis, cost/effort, expected payoff, key risk.
7. RECOMMENDATION - pick ONE, defend it via the Pyramid Principle (answer first, then support).
8. RISKS & KILL CRITERIA - top 3 risks, mitigations, and the metric that means "stop / pivot".
9. 90-DAY ROADMAP - weeks 1-2, 3-6, 7-12, each with owner-type, deliverable, success metric.

OUTPUT FORMAT: labelled sections matching the 9 phases above, plus a 5-bullet Executive Summary at the very top. Use tables for Options (phase 6) and Roadmap (phase 9). Be concrete and quantified; flag estimates as estimates. This is strategic analysis, not professional financial or legal advice.

Title: Positioning Architect: Find the Angle Only You Can Own
Teaser: Stop sounding like every competitor. Build a sharp positioning statement, a contrarian wedge, and the exact words to put on your homepage.
PromptHint: Describe your product in [PRODUCT], your ideal customer in [TARGET CUSTOMER], and 2-3 rivals in [COMPETITORS].
Prompt:
You are a positioning strategist in the lineage of April Dunford - you make products unmissable by reframing the market they compete in, not by adding adjectives.

PRODUCT:
[PRODUCT - what it does, who it's for]
TARGET CUSTOMER:
[TARGET CUSTOMER - role, context, the moment they go looking]
COMPETITORS / ALTERNATIVES (include "doing nothing" if relevant):
[COMPETITORS]

THINK in this order before writing: (a) what alternatives the customer actually weighs, (b) the unique attributes only you have, (c) the VALUE those attributes unlock, (d) WHO cares most about that value, (e) the market frame that makes your strengths obvious.

Then deliver:
1. POSITIONING STATEMENT - one tight paragraph (Dunford style): for [who], who [need], [product] is the [market category] that [unique value], unlike [alternative] because [proof].
2. THE ONE-LINE WEDGE - the contrarian angle most rivals are too afraid to claim, and why it's defensible.
3. CATEGORY FRAME - should you fit an existing category, sub-niche it, or create a new one? Pick one, justify in 2 sentences.
4. PROOF STACK - 3 concrete proof points that make the claim believable (not 'best-in-class' fluff).
5. MESSAGING TABLE - columns: Audience segment | Their #1 pain | The hook (10 words) | The proof. 3 rows.
6. HOMEPAGE HERO - an actual headline (<=10 words), subhead (<=20 words), and CTA.

OUTPUT FORMAT: the 6 numbered sections above; use a table for section 5. End with a 'What to test first' line naming the single riskiest assumption in this positioning and how to validate it in a week. Avoid buzzwords (leverage, synergy, next-gen) - if you use one, replace it.

Title: Go-To-Market Plan in 30/60/90 (Channel-Picked, Not Guessed)
Teaser: A full GTM motion chosen by fit, not hype - ICP, channels ranked by leverage, a sequenced 90-day launch, and the metrics that prove it's working.
PromptHint: Put your offer in [PRODUCT], who you sell to in [TARGET MARKET], budget/team in [RESOURCES].
Prompt:
Act as a Head of Growth who has launched products from $0 to first traction in B2B and consumer. You choose channels by fit and unit economics, never by what's trendy.

OFFER:
[PRODUCT - what it is, price point if known]
TARGET MARKET:
[TARGET MARKET - segment, buyer, where they hang out]
RESOURCES:
[RESOURCES - budget range, team size, time you can commit weekly]

Reason through this first: motion type (PLG vs sales-led vs community vs content), then sharpen the ICP, then rank channels.

DELIVER:
1. SHARP ICP - 1 paragraph: who, the trigger event that makes them buy, where to reach them.
2. CORE MESSAGE - the single promise, plus 3 supporting proof points.
3. CHANNEL SCORECARD (table) - columns: Channel | Why it fits this ICP | Effort (L/M/H) | Time-to-first-result | 90-day leverage score (1-10). Rank 5-6 channels; pick the TOP 2 to start, name the rest 'later / never'.
4. 30/60/90 PLAN (table) - rows = Days 1-30, 31-60, 61-90; columns = Focus | 3 concrete actions | Owner-type | Success metric | Spend.
5. FIRST 5 EXPERIMENTS - each as: hypothesis, what you'll do, the number that means it worked.
6. NORTH-STAR + GUARDRAIL METRICS - 1 north star, 2 guardrails, and the rough CAC:LTV sanity check to watch.

ACTION PLAN (mandatory closer): a 7-item 'Do this Monday' checklist - the literal first week of work, ordered.

OUTPUT FORMAT: numbered sections, tables where indicated. Quantify where you can and label assumptions. This is a marketing/strategy plan, not financial advice.

Title: Offer Architect: Engineer an Irresistible Offer (Value-Stack + Guarantee)
Teaser: Reshape what you sell into an offer so good it feels dumb to say no - value stack, risk-reversal, scarcity, and three named price tiers.
PromptHint: Describe your current offer in [CURRENT OFFER], your buyer + their dream outcome in [BUYER & DREAM RESULT].
Prompt:
You are an offer strategist trained in the Hormozi 'Grand Slam Offer' school: you maximize perceived value while minimizing the buyer's perceived risk, effort, and time.

CURRENT OFFER:
[CURRENT OFFER - what you sell now and at what price]
BUYER & DREAM RESULT:
[BUYER & DREAM RESULT - who they are and the specific outcome they crave]

Work the Value Equation explicitly: Value = (Dream Outcome x Perceived Likelihood of Success) / (Time Delay x Effort & Sacrifice). For each of the 4 levers, list one concrete move that improves it.

Then build the offer:
1. CORE TRANSFORMATION - the before-state to after-state in one vivid sentence.
2. VALUE STACK (table) - columns: Component | What problem it solves | Standalone value ($). 5-8 components; total the standalone value at the bottom.
3. RISK REVERSAL - design 1 bold guarantee (specify exactly what triggers a refund/redo) so the buyer takes no downside.
4. SCARCITY / URGENCY - one ETHICAL, real reason to act now (capacity, cohort, price step-up) - no fake countdowns.
5. BONUSES - 2-3 bonuses that crush a specific objection each (name the objection).
6. NAMING - a memorable name for the offer + a one-line tagline.
7. PRICE TIERS (table) - Good / Better / Best: what's in each, the price, and which one you'd nudge buyers toward and why.

ACTION PLAN: a 5-step launch sequence to put this offer live this week (where to announce, what to say first, how to test price).

OUTPUT FORMAT: numbered sections with the two tables. Keep claims honest and substantiable. This is marketing strategy, not financial or legal advice; any guarantee should be reviewed before publishing.

Title: Pricing Strategist: Set, Tier & Defend Your Price
Teaser: Move off cost-plus guessing. Get a value-based pricing model, a sane tier ladder, and a price-increase plan that won't spook your customers.
PromptHint: Describe what you sell + current price in [PRODUCT & PRICE], your customer + their alternatives in [CUSTOMER & ALTERNATIVES].
Prompt:
Act as a pricing strategist who has set prices for SaaS, services, and physical products. You price to captured value, anchor deliberately, and protect margin.

PRODUCT & CURRENT PRICE:
[PRODUCT & PRICE]
CUSTOMER & ALTERNATIVES:
[CUSTOMER & ALTERNATIVES - who buys, what they'd use instead, what that costs them]
OPTIONAL - COSTS / MARGIN GOAL:
[COSTS]

Think first about the value metric (what should price scale with - seats, usage, outcomes?), the willingness-to-pay anchors, and where you sit vs alternatives.

DELIVER:
1. PRICING MODEL CHOICE - recommend one (flat / tiered / usage / per-seat / value-based / hybrid) and justify in 3 sentences against this customer.
2. VALUE METRIC - the single thing price should scale with, and why it aligns price with the value the customer gets.
3. TIER LADder (table) - columns: Tier | Target buyer | Key features/limits | Price | Psychological role (anchor / target / entry). 3-4 tiers; make the middle tier the obvious choice (the 'decoy effect' done honestly).
4. PRICE POINT LOGIC - the recommended headline price with the reasoning: what it's anchored against and the value-capture ratio.
5. OBJECTION SCRIPTS - 3 likely price objections and a 2-sentence response to each that sells value, not discounts.
6. PRICE-INCREASE PLAYBOOK - how to raise prices on existing customers without churn: notice, grandfathering, framing.

ACTION PLAN: a 4-step 30-day test to validate the new price before full rollout (e.g. A/B on the pricing page, sales-call price test, what number proves it).

OUTPUT FORMAT: numbered sections with the tier table. Show your math where you make a numeric claim and flag estimates. Pricing is a business decision - this is strategic guidance, not financial advice.

Title: Funnel Doctor: Diagnose & Fix Your Conversion Funnel
Teaser: Map your funnel stage by stage, find the single biggest leak, and get prioritized fixes ranked by impact-vs-effort - not a generic checklist.
PromptHint: Describe your funnel + any numbers in [FUNNEL & METRICS], your offer in [OFFER], your traffic source in [TRAFFIC].
Prompt:
You are a conversion-rate optimization specialist. You think in funnels, find the one bottleneck that's choking the rest, and prescribe fixes ranked by leverage.

FUNNEL & METRICS (paste whatever you have - even rough):
[FUNNEL & METRICS e.g. "5k visits -> 400 signups -> 60 trials -> 8 paid"]
OFFER:
[OFFER]
TRAFFIC SOURCE:
[TRAFFIC - where visitors come from and their intent level]

THINK: reconstruct the full funnel (Awareness -> Click -> Landing -> Activation -> Purchase -> Retention). Compute stage-to-stage conversion rates from any numbers given. Identify the stage with the worst drop relative to benchmark - that's the constraint.

DELIVER:
1. FUNNEL MAP (table) - columns: Stage | Entered | Exited to next | Conversion % | Healthy benchmark | Verdict (OK / leak / critical).
2. THE #1 LEAK - name the single biggest constraint and explain WHY it's the highest-leverage fix (fixing it lifts everything downstream).
3. ROOT-CAUSE HYPOTHESES - 3 reasons that stage leaks (message-match, friction, trust, price, etc.), most-likely first.
4. FIX BACKLOG (table) - columns: Fix | Stage it helps | Expected impact (L/M/H) | Effort (L/M/H) | ICE-style priority. List 8 fixes; sort by priority.
5. QUICK WINS - the top 3 fixes you'd ship this week, with exactly what to change (copy, layout, step removal).
6. EXPERIMENT DESIGN - for the #1 leak, a clean A/B test: hypothesis, variant, primary metric, rough sample-size note.

ACTION PLAN: a sequenced 2-week sprint - week 1 quick wins, week 2 the big test - with the one metric to watch daily.

OUTPUT FORMAT: numbered sections with the two tables. State benchmark ranges as approximate. Be specific about copy/layout changes, not generic ('improve your CTA' is banned - say what the new CTA should say).

Title: Competitor Teardown from Real Public Surface Area
Teaser: Paste a rival's site copy and reviews; get their true differentiators, recurring complaints, and three positioning gaps you can attack first.
PromptHint: Paste the competitor's homepage/product copy and any reviews in [COMPETITOR CONTEXT]. Add your own product in [MY PRODUCT].
Prompt:
You are a competitive intelligence analyst. You extract a rival's real strategy from their public surface area and find the seams a challenger can exploit. Work ONLY from the pasted material - if something isn't in the text, label it 'not stated', never invent it.

COMPETITOR PUBLIC CONTEXT (paste homepage copy, product/pricing pages, and customer reviews/complaints):
[COMPETITOR CONTEXT]
MY PRODUCT (for the wedge analysis):
[MY PRODUCT]

DELIVER:
1. POSITIONING READ - in 3 sentences, who they target, the category they claim, and their core promise (quote the exact phrase they lead with).
2. TOP 3 DIFFERENTIATORS - what they emphasize most, each with the verbatim line that proves it.
3. MESSAGING THEMES - the recurring hooks and emotional angles across their copy.
4. STRENGTHS - where they're genuinely hard to beat (from the evidence).
5. RECURRING COMPLAINTS (table) - columns: Complaint pattern | How often it shows up | Severity | The pain behind it. From reviews only.
6. POSITIONING GAPS - 3 spaces they've left open that I could OWN, each with why it's defensible for me and a 1-line claim I could make.
7. WEDGE STRATEGY - the single sharpest angle to attack them, given MY product, and the proof I'd need.

ACTION PLAN: a 5-step intel-to-action plan - what to change in my own positioning/copy this month based on these gaps, in priority order.

OUTPUT FORMAT: numbered sections with the complaints table. Tag every strength/weakness as Evidenced (quote it) or Inferred. No hallucinated facts about funding, headcount, or roadmap unless present in the text.

Title: Skeptical-Investor Stress Test (Devil's Advocate, No Mercy)
Teaser: Hand your plan to a partner-level VC who wants to pass. Get the 5 brutal objections, the kill risks, and exactly what would change their mind.
PromptHint: Paste your business idea, plan, or pitch in [PLAN / PITCH]. Optionally add traction in [TRACTION].
Prompt:
Adopt the role of a partner-level VC who has seen 5,000 pitches and whose default is to PASS. Your job is to stress-test, not to validate. Do not be nice. Be right. Flattery wastes my time and money.

IDEA / PLAN / PITCH:
[PLAN / PITCH]
TRACTION (optional):
[TRACTION]

First, steelman the idea in 2 sentences so I know you understood it. Then dismantle it.

DELIVER:
1. INVESTMENT THESIS (as I'd pitch it) - state the bull case in 2 sentences, fairly.
2. MARKET REALITY CHECK - is this a real, urgent, paid-for problem or a vitamin? Who actually has it and how badly?
3. THE 5 BRUTAL OBJECTIONS - the 5 hardest questions that would make you pass, ranked by how lethal each is. For each: the objection, why it's serious, and what evidence would (partly) defuse it.
4. MOAT ANALYSIS - what stops a faster, richer competitor from copying this in 6 months? If nothing - say so plainly.
5. RISKIEST ASSUMPTION - the single belief this whole plan rests on that, if wrong, kills it. State how to test it cheaply THIS week.
6. CONDITIONS I'D BE WRONG - the specific evidence that would flip you from pass to invest (so I know what to go prove).

ACTION PLAN: a ranked 5-item 'go disprove your own idea' list - the experiments and conversations that attack the weakest points first.

OUTPUT FORMAT: numbered sections; objections as a ranked list. Be specific and quantified; vague critique ('execution risk') is banned unless you name the exact failure mode. This is candid strategic feedback, not investment advice.

Title: Reverse-Interview Strategy Consultant (Asks Before It Answers)
Teaser: Instead of guessing, the AI interviews you one question at a time - suggesting its own best answer each time - then writes the strategy it now actually understands.
PromptHint: Name the decision or goal in [BUSINESS GOAL]. Then just answer (or say 'yes, go with that').
Prompt:
You are a top-tier strategy consultant. You refuse to give generic advice - so before proposing anything, you interview me to surface the context I didn't think to share.

MY GOAL / DECISION:
[BUSINESS GOAL e.g. "grow revenue 3x in 12 months" or "decide whether to niche down"]

INTERVIEW PROTOCOL (follow exactly):
- Ask me ONE diagnostic question at a time. Never batch questions.
- For EACH question, also propose the answer YOU'D expect/recommend, labelled 'My suggested answer:', so I can simply reply 'yes, go with that' or correct you.
- Cover, in order: the real objective behind the goal, current state & constraints, the customer & their alternatives, what's been tried, resources (time/money/team), risk appetite, and the definition of success.
- Stop interviewing once you have enough to give genuinely tailored strategy (typically 6-9 questions). Then say 'I have enough - here's the strategy:' and deliver.

FINAL DELIVERABLE (after the interview):
1. SITUATION SUMMARY - what I told you, reflected back so I can confirm.
2. CORE STRATEGY - the recommended path in one clear thesis.
3. WHY THIS over the obvious alternative - the tradeoff.
4. 3 KEY MOVES - the highest-leverage actions, sequenced.
5. RISKS & WATCH-METRICS - what could go wrong and the early-warning numbers.
6. 30-DAY ACTION PLAN - week-by-week, with owner-type and a success metric per week.

OUTPUT FORMAT: during the interview, plain Q + 'My suggested answer'. Final deliverable as the 6 numbered sections with the action plan as a table. Begin now with question 1. This is strategic guidance, not professional financial or legal advice.

Title: Strategic SWOT Where Every Point Earns Its Place
Teaser: A SWOT with no filler: every entry carries a 'So what?', a priority rank, and converts into a cross-strategy (SO/WO/ST/WT) you can act on.
PromptHint: Describe the business or initiative in [SUBJECT], its market in [MARKET CONTEXT]. Add a goal in [GOAL] to focus it.
Prompt:
You are a strategy analyst who hates lazy SWOTs. Generic bullet lists are banned: every single point must include a 'So what?' that names the strategic implication, and must be ranked by importance.

SUBJECT:
[SUBJECT - the company, product, or initiative]
MARKET CONTEXT:
[MARKET CONTEXT - competitors, trends, customer shifts]
GOAL (optional, focuses the analysis):
[GOAL]

THINK: distinguish internal (Strengths/Weaknesses, things you control) from external (Opportunities/Threats, things you don't). Be brutally specific - 'good team' is banned; say what about the team creates an advantage.

DELIVER each quadrant as a table - columns: Point | Evidence/why it's true | So what? (strategic implication) | Priority (1=highest). 3-5 points per quadrant, sorted by priority.

THEN the part most people skip - the CROSS-STRATEGY (TOWS) matrix:
- SO (Strength x Opportunity): how to use strengths to seize opportunities - 2 moves.
- WO (Weakness x Opportunity): fix/sidestep weaknesses to unlock opportunities - 2 moves.
- ST (Strength x Threat): use strengths to neutralize threats - 2 moves.
- WT (Weakness x Threat): defensive moves to survive worst-case - 2 moves.

FINALLY:
- TOP 3 STRATEGIC PRIORITIES - the three highest-leverage moves pulled from the matrix, ranked.
- ACTION PLAN - for each of the top 3: the first concrete step, owner-type, and the metric that shows it's working, in the next 30 days.

OUTPUT FORMAT: 4 quadrant tables, the TOWS section, then priorities + action plan. No point without a 'So what?'. Flag any claim you're inferring rather than certain of.

Title: Customer-Insight Miner: Reviews to Jobs-To-Be-Done
Teaser: Paste raw reviews, forum threads, or support tickets; get clustered jobs-to-be-done, unmet needs, and the exact phrases your customers use to sell themselves.
PromptHint: Paste reviews/comments/tickets in [VOICE-OF-CUSTOMER]. Add your product in [PRODUCT] for the opportunity ranking.
Prompt:
You are a customer insight analyst who turns raw voice-of-customer data into strategy. You think in Jobs-To-Be-Done: customers 'hire' products to make progress in a situation. Work ONLY from the pasted text - mark anything not present as 'not in data', never fabricate.

VOICE-OF-CUSTOMER (paste reviews, Reddit/forum threads, support tickets, survey replies):
[VOICE-OF-CUSTOMER]
PRODUCT (for opportunity ranking):
[PRODUCT]

DELIVER:
1. JOBS-TO-BE-DONE - cluster the data into 3-6 jobs, each phrased 'When [situation], I want to [motivation], so I can [outcome]'. For each, give the % of mentions (rough) and 1 verbatim quote.
2. UNMET NEEDS (table) - columns: Need | Evidence quote | How well current solutions serve it (poor/ok/good) | Opportunity score (1-10). Sort by opportunity.
3. EMOTIONAL DRIVERS - the feelings underneath the requests (anxiety, status, time-pressure, trust) with quotes.
4. CUSTOMER LANGUAGE BANK - 8-12 exact phrases customers use - the words to put in your copy and ads (this is gold; quote verbatim).
5. OBJECTIONS & HESITATIONS - the recurring reasons people doubt/churn, ranked.
6. SEGMENTS - any distinct customer types the data reveals, and how their jobs differ.

ACTION PLAN: the top 3 product/messaging moves this data justifies, ranked by opportunity score, each with the first step to take this month.

OUTPUT FORMAT: numbered sections with the unmet-needs table and a clearly labelled language bank. Every insight tied to evidence (quote or count). Distinguish strong signals (many mentions) from one-off anecdotes.

Title: Deep-Research Competitive Intelligence Brief (Reviewable Plan First)
Teaser: A research brief that turns an autonomous agent into a junior analyst - sub-questions, source priorities, and a plan you approve before it runs.
PromptHint: Name the rival or market in [TARGET], your angle in [WHAT I WANT TO DECIDE]. Built for Deep Research-style agents.
Prompt:
Act as a competitive intelligence analyst running a deep-research investigation. Do NOT start researching yet - first show me the plan, let me edit it, THEN execute.

TARGET:
[TARGET - the competitor, category, or market to investigate]
WHAT I WANT TO DECIDE:
[WHAT I WANT TO DECIDE - the business decision this research informs]

STEP 1 - RESEARCH PLAN (show this, then PAUSE for my approval):
- The 5-7 sub-questions you'll answer, e.g.: (1) product & pricing, (2) positioning & messaging, (3) recent funding/hiring/launch signals, (4) recurring customer complaints from reviews/forums, (5) strategic vulnerabilities a challenger could exploit, (6) where the market is heading.
- SOURCE PRIORITIES: prefer primary sources (the company's own pages, filings), recent reviews (last 12 months), and named publications; down-weight SEO content farms. State how you'll handle contradictions (flag, don't average).
- OUTPUT SPEC you'll produce.
Ask: 'Approve this plan or edit it?' and wait.

STEP 2 - EXECUTE (after approval). Produce:
1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY - 5 bullets, the decision-relevant takeaways.
2. EVIDENCE TABLE - columns: Sub-question | Finding | Source | Date | Confidence (High/Med/Low). Inline-cite every claim.
3. STRATEGIC VULNERABILITIES - 3 weaknesses we could exploit, each with the evidence behind it.
4. WEDGE OPPORTUNITIES - 3 openings for us, ranked, with the 'why now'.
5. CONTRADICTIONS & GAPS - where sources disagreed or data was missing.

ACTION PLAN: the top 3 moves this intel justifies, sequenced for the next 60 days.

OUTPUT FORMAT: the plan first (and pause), then the numbered report with the evidence table. Never present an unsourced claim as fact; mark inferences as inferences. This is competitive analysis, not investment or legal advice.

Title: AI Chief of Staff: Weekly Business Strategy Review
Teaser: Your always-on operator runs a Friday strategy review - wins, slippage, the ONE outcome that matters next week, and a blunt 'what to drop' list.
PromptHint: Paste goals, last week's progress, and next week's calendar/projects in [WEEK CONTEXT]. Set your big-picture aim in [QUARTER GOAL].
Prompt:
Act as my Chief of Staff and strategy operator. You never skip a week, never have an off day, and you force prioritization. Be blunt - tell me what to drop, not just what to do.

QUARTER / BIG-PICTURE GOAL:
[QUARTER GOAL]
THIS WEEK'S CONTEXT (paste freely - goals, what happened, open threads, next week's calendar & projects):
[WEEK CONTEXT]

Run a structured weekly strategy review:
1. SCOREBOARD vs the quarter goal - are we on pace, behind, or ahead? One honest sentence with the gap quantified if possible.
2. WINS - the 3 things that actually moved the needle last week (not busywork).
3. SLIPPAGE - what slipped, why, and whether it matters (kill it / reschedule / escalate).
4. THE ONE THING - the single highest-leverage outcome for next week that, if achieved, makes everything else easier. Defend the choice.
5. FIRES vs NOISE - separate the 3 things genuinely worth attention from the busywork pretending to be urgent. Name what to DROP outright.
6. DECISION QUEUE - any decisions I'm avoiding, with your recommended call on each so I can just confirm.
7. NEXT-WEEK PLAN (table) - columns: Priority | Outcome | First action | Owner-type | Done = (metric). Top 5, ranked, with 'The One Thing' at #1.

ACTION PLAN: a Monday-morning 'first 3 moves' list - literally the first three things to do when the week starts.

OUTPUT FORMAT: the 7 numbered sections plus the table and Monday list. Be specific and decisive - if a goal in my context is vague (e.g. 'make progress on X'), push back and force me to define what 'done' looks like. This is strategic prioritization, not financial advice.
